excerpt_text: We use unit tests and some BLEU scores but they catch nothing about hallucination.
The model confidently states wrong things. That's our biggest worry.
Users ask why the system said X and we genuinely cannot tell them.
Provider went down for four hours and so did our product completely.
We set thresholds by gut feel. There's no industry standard to reference.
Changing one word in the prompt dropped accuracy by twelve percent.
HIPAA means we need near-zero PII leakage. The default tools don't give us that.
We have five different monitoring tools and none of them talk to each other.
The retrieval system pulled the wrong document and the model cited it confidently.
Our benchmark scores are great but users keep complaining about wrong answers.
We cannot explain to the regulator why the system made that recommendation.
Every model update requires us to re-validate the prompt from scratch.
The audit log exists but it's not structured so we can't query it meaningfully.
A confidence score would help but the model just asserts everything equally.
GDPR means we need to explain automated decisions. We currently cannot do that.
Cost went up three times when we moved to the better model. Quality is hard to quantify.
Our medical team wants 99% factual precision. The 85% default is nowhere near enough.
We discovered the system was using training data instead of the provided document.
Two weeks after the EU AI Act passed we had an emergency threshold review.
The fragmentation is exhausting. Prometheus for infra, custom scripts for semantics.
No citation means users cannot verify anything. Trust just collapses.
We had a P95 latency spike to eight seconds. Our SLA is two.
Prompt version control is a spreadsheet. It breaks constantly.
The financial regulator asked for decision logs. We had nothing machine-readable.
Good scores on HELM but still getting hallucinations daily in production.
Every system is different so a generic framework wouldn't work for us. Or so we thought.
The explanation the model gives often contradicts what it actually did.
We lost a client because the system cited a regulation that was repealed.
Our GPU cluster has variance in latency that the LLM API doesn't.
There is no transparency by default. You have to build it in deliberately.
